name: S1 17.9 - JR (w2 0.2E)

name: S1 20.1 - w2 0.1N
details: w2 0.1N water, a cement trough in a corral that appears to be overflowing. No cows present in image. Mandatory unbridged crossing of Rio Grande within White Rock Canyon. Suggested fording location is at bottom of Frijoles Overlook Trail, at a wide-ish spot where river can be up to 300ft across (but shallower and with less current than elsewhere esp upstream). River here is under influence from Cochiti Reservoir downstream. Muddy banks common. Avoid during times of high run-off (CW consider beginning hike in White Rock instead of SF).

name: S1 27.6 - Rio Grande Ford
details: Rio Grande Ford Check USGS streamflow gage reports for Otowi Bridge (along NM 502 too far / illegal to walk upstream) before starting from Santa Fe. Swimming or an inflatable raft / kayak have historically been required during avg. May / June flow. WB turn around if unsafe! Return toward SF, then RTD bus to White Rock and backtrack via WR Alt Rte to other side of Rio to connect footsteps minus the crossing. (CCW can also use Alt to do same in reverse.) Seasonal water in Frijoles Canyon here, flowing through its alluvial fan near confluence with Rio Grande. XC ends hereabouts.

name: S1 28.2 - W1 (XC>TR)
details: W1 (XC>TR) Semi-informal River Trail continues NE, again near banks of Rio, back from riverside brush. Seasonal water where River Trail crosses Ancho Canyon. Good alternative to drinking from the Rio Grande. Just beyond is jct w/ Ancho Rapids Tr, which main route follows N soon climbing out of White Rock Cyn on established trail. White Rock Resupply Loop

name: S1 30.7 - w2 (S1B START)
details: w2 (S1B START) instead stays with River Trail, which itself continues to improve heading NE, then joins other established trails up to village of White Rock. Very scenic albeit considerably longer than main route - only really recommended if intending to resupply or to send home a packraft after fording Rio. Trailhead for Frey Trail in Juniper Campground. name: S1 40.8 - HQ W3
details: HQ W3 and avoid the need to camp, which is good since you can't camp in canyon bottoms anyway). You should also pay the entrance fee while at the VC ($15 per indv arriving on foot., good for a week). Shuttles stop in the parking lot seasonally and run to village of White Rock, providing an alternative way to resupply vs walking into town via the Resupply Loop - useful if you took the Frijoles RIM Alt into the park to get here).
